name: DappCon (Berlin Blockchain Week)
edition: DappCon 2025 (6th edition), the cornerstone/flagship event of Berlin Blockchain Week 2025 (BBW ran ~June 8-22, 2025)
attendance: ~800-1,000+ builders/attendees, 80+ speakers, 15+ sessions (sources vary: Coinpedia cites 800+ attendees/80+ speakers; multiple listings cite 900+ builders; one Gnosis-ecosystem recap cites 1,000+ builders and 100+ speakers). Hosted/organized by Gnosis.
defi_themes: DEX / AMM liquidity infrastructure and capital efficiency (Balancer V3 modular architecture, ReCLAMM concentrated liquidity)
Lending protocol evolution and multi-protocol capital coordination (Aave V4)
Protocol composability and liquidity routing across DeFi venues (Aave + Balancer + KPK)
Regulated / compliant stablecoins and on-chain fiat (Monerium EURe)
Smart-contract customization and extensibility for DeFi pools (Balancer V3 Hooks)
Open financial tools and open infrastructure for an 'Open Internet' (overarching conference theme)
DeFi as a core pillar alongside scalability, privacy, and user adoption

notable_panels.title: Fireside Chat: Aave V4
notable_panels.topic: Multi-protocol coordination and capital efficiency across Aave, Balancer and KPK; how protocols align when capital efficiency is the goal, Aave V4 technical upgrades affecting liquidity routing, and Balancer V3's modular architecture. June 17, 10:30-11:00, Consensus Layer (HALLE).
notable_panels.speakers: Stani Kulechov (Aave founder), Marcus (Balancer Labs CEO), Claberus/Marcelo (KPK)

notable_panels.title: ReCLAMMs: A Passive Approach to Concentrated Liquidity
notable_panels.topic: A new approach to concentrated liquidity that minimizes overhead without giving up precision. June 17, 08:30-08:50, Consensus Layer (HALLE).

notable_panels.title: Balancer V3 Hooks in Action (Workshop)
notable_panels.topic: Practical examples of Balancer V3 Hooks: customizing pool behavior, implementing access control, and experimenting with logic extensions. June 18, 11:45-12:45, Execution Layer (Workshop Room).

notable_panels.title: Building Regulated Stablecoins
notable_panels.topic: Issuing compliant, regulated stablecoins / on-chain fiat (Monerium EURe e-money model). June 17, DappCon25.
notable_panels.speakers: Monerium (speaker listed by Monerium as a DappCon25 session)

key_takeaways: Capital efficiency was the headline DeFi theme: leading lending (Aave V4) and AMM/DEX (Balancer V3) protocols framed the next cycle around multi-protocol coordination and shared liquidity rather than siloed pools.
AMM design is shifting toward 'passive' concentrated liquidity (Balancer's ReCLAMM) that aims to retain precision while reducing LP management overhead.
Extensibility/customization (Balancer V3 Hooks) signals DeFi infrastructure becoming modular and developer-programmable at the pool level.

Regulated stablecoins / on-chain fiat (Monerium EURe) featured as a compliance-forward bridge between TradFi and DeFi, reflecting the broader 2025 push toward institutionally legible, regulated euro/dollar settlement rails.

DeFi was positioned as one of the core pillars of the conference's 'Applications & Infrastructure for an Open Internet' theme, alongside open-source infrastructure, scalability, privacy and user adoption.

Tokenized real-world assets had representation via Backed Finance (Adam Levi), consistent with 2025's broad RWA growth narrative, though DappCon's DeFi programming skewed more toward DEX/lending infrastructure than RWA.
